summary.intent: Address a second Codex automated review finding on PR #668 in fogodev/ars-ui, specifically that no-op `Reset` and equal-value `SetPressed` transitions on ToggleButton were incorrectly reporting `state_changed=true`.
summary.outcome: Fixed `value_change_plan` helper to return a no-op `TransitionPlan` when the target pressed value equals the current state, added a regression test, updated the spec guard text, passed the full mutation gate (62 caught, 0 missed), and committed as a second Codex review fix on PR #668.

repo: ToggleButton state transitions flow through a `value_change_plan` helper that returns a `TransitionPlan`; returning `TransitionPlan::to(target)` on equal values incorrectly signals `state_changed=true`.
The project uses `cargo mutants` as a mutation testing gate scoped to individual files; 83 mutants were generated for toggle_button/mod.rs, with ~21 typically unviable due to compile errors.
Spec validation is run via `cargo xtask spec validate` and must stay in sync with implementation changes, including guard text snippets in `spec/components/utility/toggle-button.md`.
Coverage is measured with `cargo llvm-cov` outputting LCOV format; toggle_button/mod.rs reached 94.91% line coverage after the fix.
Codex review flow uses GitHub reactions (👀 = in-progress, 👍 = approved) on the `@codex review` trigger comment, polled via `gh api repos/.../issues/comments/.../reactions`.
Review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation using the thread node ID (prefix `PRRT_`).

code.finding: `value_change_plan` was returning `TransitionPlan::to(target)` even when `pressed.get() == next`, causing no-op transitions to report `state_changed=true`. Fixed to return a no-op plan on equal values.
Spec guard text for `value_change_plan` idempotency needed updating to match the new no-op contract after the implementation fix.

workflow: Mutation testing with `cargo mutants` scoped to a single file is slow (multiple minutes for 83 mutants) but provides high confidence for state-machine helpers; run it after any state-logic change.
The Codex review loop requires polling both reactions on the trigger comment and unresolved review threads in parallel to detect approval vs. new findings promptly.
`cargo fmt --check` should be run before committing; formatting diffs were caught post-fix and required an extra `cargo fmt` pass.
Pushing before triggering `@codex review` is required — Codex reviews the pushed HEAD, not local state.
Inline reply + thread resolution + new `@codex review` trigger is the correct three-step sequence after each fix push.

friction: Mutation gate is slow (~several minutes for 83 mutants), requiring multiple polling intervals before confirming the fix was fully mutation-tested.
`cargo fmt --check` was not run until after the fix was otherwise complete, requiring an extra formatting pass and rerun of focused tests.
Codex review polling requires multiple separate API calls (reactions, PR checks, GraphQL thread query) with no single unified status endpoint, adding loop complexity.
The initial `TransitionPlan::to(target)` pattern was used elsewhere in the codebase, making it easy to introduce the same no-op reporting bug in similar helpers.
open_items: CI coverage and feature-flag matrix jobs were still pending when the session ended; final green CI status not confirmed in transcript.
Codex second-pass approval (👍 reaction) was not yet received when the commit was made; the trigger was re-posted but approval outcome is unknown.
Other state-machine helpers using `TransitionPlan::to(target)` in the repo may have the same no-op reporting bug and should be audited.
The mutation gate for the first Codex fix (commit 2df6c58d) may not have been rerun after the second fix changed overlapping code paths.
